article_meta.title: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11

reasoning: Mostly neutral and uses attribution (e.g., “the article suggests,” “reportedly”) to distance loaded claims. Minor slippage appears with “Despite these efforts” and “fans overwhelmingly preferred,” which echo the source’s framing. Overall, it avoids adopting the most charged language and attributes the “rabidly anti-Trump” label to the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses a few interpretive phrases like “overwhelmingly preferred” but otherwise neutral verbs and clear attribution.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Contested claims are hedged with “reportedly”/“allegedly”; most statements are presented as from the article.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: “Despite these efforts” implies endorsement of the article’s claim about TMZ favoring Bad Bunny.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids emotive escalation and attributes the inflammatory label to the article.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s own voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions beyond attributed assertions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Slightly echoes the article’s slant against TMZ/Bad Bunny but maintains some distance via attribution.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: No major perspectives omitted from the source; minor omission of TMZ’s own viewpoint.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited use of subjective terms like “overwhelmingly.”
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically loaded label is clearly attributed to the article, not adopted.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Slight emphasis on negative outcomes for Bad Bunny/TMZ, but not excessive.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No clear deference to elites or populist framing.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Generally more neutral than the source with small lapses.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Lists only Breitbart; omits TMZ/fans as distinct perspectives mentioned in the article.
